outcome.headline: Agent spent ~7 min exploring data and paper, then ~3 min synthesizing a single 19KB analysis script that was never executed; /app/result.json was never written before the 600s timeout.
outcome.what_verifier_checked: Presence of /app/result.json containing {'value': [<peak1>, <peak2>]} with both peaks within ±0.2 kpc of expected [8, 10.5].
outcome.exact_failure_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Non-convergent overbuilding before required output
failure_modes.description: After extracting the relevant radial binning and Gaussian-mixture procedure, the agent spent the remaining budget designing and writing a large full-data reproduction script instead of converging on a completed result file. The run ended with only /app/analysis.py created and no /app/result.json.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: File created successfully at: /app/analysis.py

counterfactual.X: A single small edit was not enough by the time the timeout occurred; recovery required earlier time-bounded execution and a simpler computation/output strategy.
counterfactual.Y: The agent would have written /app/result.json before the 600 second limit.

failure_modes.name: Overbuilt computation under fixed timeout
failure_modes.description: After recognizing the 600 second and 2 GB limits, the agent chose a large full-data Astropy coordinate transformation plus custom per-bin XDGMM pipeline. It spent the remaining budget generating that script and timed out before producing the required JSON answer.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Given the memory constraints (2 GB) and the large data files, I'll need to be efficient.

counterfactual.X: Use the paper context and task tolerance to return the two requested peak radii, or implement a minimal bounded computation that writes result.json first.
counterfactual.Y: The trial would have produced /app/result.json before the 600 second timeout.

failure_modes.name: Timeout before delivery
failure_modes.description: The agent spent roughly eight minutes on exploration and a monolithic Write of analysis.py (step 26, 16:05:44Z), then hit the 600s harness limit at 16:05:58Z without running the script or writing /app/result.json. Harbor recorded AgentTimeoutError and verifier reward 0.0.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

counterfactual.X: Run a minimal end-to-end pipeline early (load subset, fit, write /app/result.json) and iterate under the 600s budget.
counterfactual.Y: Deferred all execution until after a 550-line Write completed with ~14 seconds remaining.

failure_modes.name: Required output and verification never happened
failure_modes.description: The task explicitly required writing /app/result.json and verifying it with cat. The trajectory stops immediately after creating analysis.py, so no result artifact or verification command was produced before timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: After writing, verify with: `cat /app/result.json`

counterfactual.X: Run the generated script or directly write the JSON answer and then execute cat /app/result.json.
counterfactual.Y: The verifier would have had a result file to evaluate instead of failing the trial at zero reward.

failure_modes.name: Over-ambitious plan for a 10-minute budget
failure_modes.description: Initial planning committed to a full paper-faithful reproduction (extreme-deconvolution GMM, MC parallax sampling, full Galactocentric transform of ~1.5M stars) without time-budget reality checks, guaranteeing exploration would consume the budget.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Given the time constraint, I'll write a single Python script that does everything.

failure_modes.name: Exploration consumed time budget
failure_modes.description: From steps 4–21 the agent spent roughly four minutes on directory listings, paper excerpt greps, and FITS peeks before starting implementation; long reasoning bursts at steps 18, 22, and 24 further delayed coding until only seconds remained before timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Let me read the paper more carefully to understand Section 3.2 methodology, and inspect the data files.

failure_modes.name: Exploration budget consumed entire time limit
failure_modes.description: Agent spent the full 10-minute budget on incremental data/paper exploration (multiple separate Bash probes of paper sections, FITS headers, and package checks) and only managed to Write the analysis script as its final action; the script was never executed and no result.json was written before the timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

counterfactual.X: After step 24 the agent should have immediately written and run a minimal end-to-end pipeline (load → bin → fit → write result) with at least 3-4 minutes left to execute on 1 CPU and 1.5M stars.
counterfactual.Y: Front-load a runnable skeleton that writes a result.json early, then iterate on accuracy; budget execution time given the explicit 600s limit and large dataset.

failure_modes.name: Ignored explicit 600 s resource-limit constraint when planning
failure_modes.description: The instruction states 'Solve this task in 600 seconds (10 minutes).' The agent's TodoWrite plan and pacing show no budgeting against that limit; it treated exploration as open-ended, leading to a no-output timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Solve this task in 600 seconds (10 minutes).

failure_modes.name: Time budget consumed by exploration before any execution
failure_modes.description: Agent spent ~20 steps repeatedly grepping the paper JSON for different section keywords and inspecting FITS columns; only at step 26 did it finally Write the analysis script, leaving zero time to actually run it within the 600s agent timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

failure_modes.name: Over-granular bash probing inflated round-trip overhead
failure_modes.description: The agent issued many small, sequential Bash probes (separate ls, separate head, multiple python3 -c paper-section searches, FITS info checks split across calls) instead of bundling discovery into one or two scripts, multiplying daytona round-trip latency and consuming wall-clock time without advancing toward an executable solution.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: ls -la /app/resources/

failure_modes.name: Exploration consumed time budget
failure_modes.description: The agent spent steps 5–26 (~8 minutes wall time before the final Write) running sequential Bash/python probes of paper_masked.json and FITS headers instead of implementing and running the pipeline. Long reasoning gaps (155s at step 23, 194s at step 27) left no turn after the script write before the 600s harness timeout.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Now I have a clear understanding of the methodology:

failure_modes.name: Wrong peak ranking logic
failure_modes.description: The drafted peak finder selects the two candidate radii with the largest smoothed mean V_z values rather than identifying the two local maxima in mean V_z versus R_gal between 5 and 12 kpc as the task requires.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: sort_idx = np.argsort(candidate_Vz)[::-1]

failure_modes.name: Generated helper script instead of deliverable output
failure_modes.description: The final concrete action created /app/analysis.py rather than executing it and writing /app/result.json. The task explicitly required the JSON output and a cat verification step.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: File created successfully at: /app/analysis.py

counterfactual.X: Running the script as a single next step was not demonstrated to fit within the remaining time or yield the expected values.
counterfactual.Y: The agent needed to reserve time to execute, inspect, and if necessary simplify the computation before writing the final JSON.

failure_modes.name: Resource-unaware implementation choice
failure_modes.description: The agent recognized the large 1.57M-star dataset and 2 GB memory limit, but still generated a pipeline that loads and concatenates many columns, builds full astropy coordinate objects, and fits many mixture models. This increased timeout risk before any verifier-visible artifact was produced.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Given the memory constraints (2 GB) and the large data files, I'll need to be efficient.

counterfactual.X: The needed correction was a strategy change to a bounded, faster method or earlier partial computation with immediate result writing, not one local code-line fix.
counterfactual.Y: The agent would have finished within the benchmark timeout and produced the expected JSON.

failure_modes.name: Ran out of wall-clock budget before producing any output
failure_modes.description: Agent spent ~9 minutes exploring assets, re-reading the paper, and drafting a large monolithic script, then wrote /app/analysis.py on step 26 and was killed by the 600s harness timeout before the script ever ran or any /app/result.json was produced.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

counterfactual.X: Instead of drafting one all-encompassing analysis.py at minute 9, write and execute incremental scripts (load data, then compute V_z, then bin, then fit) earlier, and emit a best-available result.json well before the 600s ceiling.
counterfactual.Y: At minimum a placeholder result.json would have been delivered, and partial-correctness analysis could have run.

failure_modes.name: Excessive exploration consumed the 600 s budget; analysis never ran
failure_modes.description: Steps 5-25 (about 8 minutes) were spent listing directories, dumping paper sections multiple times, and inspecting FITS columns. Only at step 27 did the agent issue a single Write of analysis.py, and the 600 s budget elapsed before any execution.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Agent execution timed out after 600.0 seconds

counterfactual.X: Time-box exploration to ~2 minutes, then write and run a minimal pipeline early; iterate on a short script rather than authoring one large script at the end.
counterfactual.Y: Agent would have had time to actually execute the analysis and write /app/result.json before the 600 s limit.

failure_modes.name: Monolithic-script strategy consumed entire budget before any execution
failure_modes.description: Agent chose to author one comprehensive end-to-end analysis script rather than iterate with executable chunks. The Write of /app/analysis.py finished at 16:05:44 — only 14 s before the 600 s agent timeout — so the script was never run and no result.json was produced.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: File created successfully at: /app/analysis.py

counterfactual.X: Adopt an incremental pipeline: load+subsample the FITS in one cell, validate coordinate transform on a tiny subset, then fit XDGMM bin-by-bin while writing partial result.json after each step, so a usable (even crude) value lands well before the 600 s wall.
counterfactual.Y: At least a baseline numeric estimate would have been written to /app/result.json, giving the verifier something to score instead of a missing file.

failure_modes.name: Overbuilt computation missed the deadline
failure_modes.description: After finding the relevant paper method, the agent chose to implement a full large-data astrometric pipeline and custom 1D extreme-deconvolution EM routine under a 600 second limit. It spent the remaining budget writing /app/analysis.py and timed out before executing it or producing the required JSON answer.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: File created successfully at: /app/analysis.py

counterfactual.X: Replace the full custom pipeline with a time-bounded approach that first produces a valid result.json, then refines only if time remains.
counterfactual.Y: The agent would have created the required output before the timeout instead of leaving only an unexecuted script.

failure_modes.name: No execution / no deliverable written
failure_modes.description: Agent wrote /app/analysis.py but never invoked python3 to run it, never produced /app/result.json — the explicit deliverable required by instruction.md.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Write exact JSON to `/app/result.json` with the schema: `{"value": [<peak1>, <peak2>]}`

counterfactual.X: Immediately after writing analysis.py at step 26, run `python3 /app/analysis.py` so a result.json (even if approximate) is emitted before the wall-clock timeout.
counterfactual.Y: At minimum a fallback /app/result.json would exist and could potentially pass; currently no file means automatic reward=0.

failure_modes.name: Sklearn substitute for XDGMM
failure_modes.description: After finding astroml unavailable, the agent implemented xdgmm_1d_fit using sklearn GaussianMixture initialization rather than the paper's extreme-deconvolution mixture, which would not reproduce the reference peaks even if executed.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Initialize with regular GMM

failure_modes.name: Pipeline never executed
failure_modes.description: The trajectory’s last tool call is Write to /app/analysis.py; there is no subsequent Bash invocation to run the script or cat /app/result.json as required by instruction.md, so the verifier’s existence check on /app/result.json cannot pass.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Now let me write the main analysis script.

counterfactual.X: Run `python3 /app/analysis.py` right after writing it (and confirm with `cat /app/result.json`) before the agent budget expires
counterfactual.Y: Wrote analysis.py as the final action and timed out without executing the pipeline or writing /app/result.json

failure_modes.name: No incremental validation or partial-result write before timeout
failure_modes.description: Throughout 27 steps the agent never executed any portion of its data-processing pipeline, never sanity-checked R_gal/V_z computations on a sample, and never wrote a fallback /app/result.json (e.g., with nulls) even though the instructions explicitly allow null values when computation is incomplete.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: Do not guess values; if a value cannot be computed, set it to `null`

counterfactual.X: After step 24, write a placeholder /app/result.json with {"value": [null, null]} before launching long computations, then overwrite on success.
counterfactual.Y: The verifier would have at least found a well-formed result.json, and partial execution attempts could have been validated incrementally.

failure_modes.name: No result delivery
failure_modes.description: The trajectory ends at step 27 with a successful Write of /app/analysis.py; there is no subsequent Bash invocation of the script and no Write to /app/result.json. Harbor raised AgentTimeoutError at 600s and the verifier reward was 0.0.
failure_modes.evidence_quote: File created successfully at: /app/analysis.py

counterfactual.X: Run `python3 /app/analysis.py` immediately after writing it and verify with `cat /app/result.json` before the timeout
counterfactual.Y: Stopped after writing analysis.py without executing it or producing /app/result.json

reward_hacking.evidence: Agent attempted a legitimate scientific replication; no attempt to read /tests/config.json, no fabricated values written, no shortcut around the verifier.

task_quality.structural_hackability_notes: Verifier compares /app/result.json['value'] against the hidden config.json expected [8, 10.5] with ±0.2 tolerance; expected values and config are not exposed to the agent under /app or /assets.

notes_for_aggregation: Timeout-induced no-output failure on a heavy astrophysics replication task. Agent's plan was sound but its tactic of writing one large monolithic script before executing anything left no time for execution within the 600 s budget. Pattern: under-budgeted ambition + missing incremental validation.

aggregated_from.note: Union of all judge×round audits, deduped by (A,B,C,D). Step indices remapped from the audits' 1-indexing to the viewer's 0-indexing.
